task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named NoteContract.kt open in the editor. It should be in the com.example.notesapp.provider package and define the authority 'com.example.notesapp.provider', a CONTENT_URI, and constants for ID, title, content, and timestamp columns.
Look for a file named NoteContentProvider.kt open in the editor. It should extend ContentProvider and define a UriMatcher that handles both directory (all items) and single item URIs.

task_completion.description: Implement standard provider methods linked to NoteDatabase
task_completion.visual_evidence: In NoteContentProvider.kt, look for the implementation of onCreate, query, insert, update, delete, and getType methods. These methods should contain logic that interacts with NoteDatabase or NoteDao (e.g., calling DAO methods to fetch or modify data).

task_completion.description: Register the provider in AndroidManifest.xml as exported
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for AndroidManifest.xml open in the editor. It must contain a <provider> tag with android:name pointing to the NoteContentProvider, android:authorities='com.example.notesapp.provider', and android:exported='true'.
Look for a terminal window or the Android Studio Build tool window showing the execution of './gradlew assembleDebug' (or a similar build command) and a 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' message.

integrity.description: Code is written in actual files, not just printed in the terminal
integrity.visual_evidence: The Kotlin and XML files should be visible in an IDE (like Android Studio) or a text editor, rather than just being echoed or cat-ed in a terminal.

integrity.description: The build success message is from a genuine Gradle process
integrity.visual_evidence: The build output should show typical Gradle logs (e.g., task execution times, daemon status) rather than a simple echoed 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' string.

integrity.description: Provider methods actually interact with the database
integrity.visual_evidence: The query, insert, update, and delete methods in NoteContentProvider.kt should call methods on the NoteDatabase or NoteDao instance, rather than returning hardcoded cursors or dummy integer values.

The project uses Android Gradle Plugin 8.2.0, Kotlin 1.9.22, and Room 2.6.1.
